stories.text: As an operations engineer, I want to add a new node to the cluster and have it sync automatically so that scaling out does not require manual data migration.
As an operations engineer, I want to choose a sync topology (full-mesh, ring, custom) so that I can balance sync traffic against convergence speed.
As an operations engineer, I want to configure the sync interval so that I can trade freshness against network bandwidth.
As an operations engineer, I want a failed sync to be retryable without risk of data corruption so that network glitches do not require manual intervention.

personas.name: Aion Engine
personas.role: Running workflows across multiple nodes for availability

stories.text: As the Aion engine, I want workflow event histories to be replicated across nodes so that a node failure does not lose workflow state.
As the Aion engine, I want to request strong consistency for critical writes so that quorum acknowledgment guarantees the event survives any single node failure.
As the Aion engine, I want eventual consistency for non-critical reads so that read latency is not penalised by sync round-trips.
As an application developer, I want to read from any node and get eventually-consistent data so that I can distribute reads across the cluster for throughput.
As an application developer, I want divergent writes to be merged automatically so that I do not need to implement my own conflict resolution for multi-writer scenarios.
As an application developer, I want to choose the conflict resolution policy per-operation so that different data types can use different merge strategies.
As a debugging developer, I want to compare root hashes between two nodes for a specific shard so that I can verify whether they are in sync.
As a debugging developer, I want sync operations to report how many nodes were transferred so that I can understand the magnitude of divergence.
